name: devflow-tdd-enforcer
description: Enforces TDD order in TASKS.md - Tests MUST be marked complete before Implementation tasks. Blocks violations in real-time.
capabilities: Detects when implementation tasks are marked complete before test tasks using regex patterns
Blocks file saves that violate TDD order with exit code 2
Supports skip conditions via @skip-tdd-check comment, session skip, or SKIP_TDD_ENFORCER=1 env var
Validates TASKS.md files in devflow/requirements/**/ directory
Provides clear blocking messages with required actions and constitutional basis
author: Dimon94
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with YAML frontmatter and markdown. No executable code, no network calls, no file system access beyond Claude Code defaults. Static findings are false positives triggered by markdown code blocks (backticks) and documentation text. Behavior matches stated purpose exactly.
valueStatement: Teams often mark implementation tasks complete before tests, breaking Test-First Development principles. This guardrail blocks real-time violations by detecting when implementation tasks are checked off before corresponding test tasks.
userTitle: Enforce TDD order in task management
limitations: Only validates TASKS.md files, not actual test files on disk
Cannot verify test quality or coverage beyond presence in task list
Requires Claude Code's PreToolUse hook mechanism to function
Does not generate test templates or implementation code

useCases.title: Enforce testing standards
useCases.description: Ensure all developers follow TDD principles across large teams by blocking policy violations before they occur

useCases.title: Maintain code quality gates
useCases.description: Automatically enforce test-first development as a prerequisite for merging implementation tasks

useCases.title: Build testing habits
useCases.description: Stay disciplined about writing tests first without relying on willpower alone

promptTemplates.title: Quick enforcement check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if my TASKS.md follows TDD order. Tell me which tasks are in the wrong sequence.

promptTemplates.title: Understand blocking reason
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain why my TASKS.md edit was blocked. Show me the correct TDD order.

promptTemplates.title: Skip TDD check
promptTemplates.prompt: Add @skip-tdd-check marker to my TASKS.md so I can proceed with this hotfix.

promptTemplates.title: Review task sequence
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my current TASKS.md and verify all test tasks are marked complete before corresponding implementation tasks.

bestPractices: Keep test tasks immediately before implementation tasks with sequential numbering
Use @skip-tdd-check comments for documented exceptions like hotfixes
Review the blocking message for constitutional basis and required actions
antiPatterns: Marking implementation tasks complete without corresponding test verification
Removing test tasks entirely to bypass TDD enforcement
Skipping TDD checks routinely instead of following the workflow

faq.question: Does this skill run test files?
faq.answer: No. This skill only validates TASKS.md task ordering. It does not execute or validate actual test files.

faq.question: Can I bypass TDD enforcement?
faq.answer: Yes. Three ways: session skip once, add @skip-tdd-check comment, or set SKIP_TDD_ENFORCER=1 environment variable.

faq.question: Does this work with all test frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill operates at task list level only. It is agnostic to Jest, pytest, or any testing framework.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Safe. This is a configuration-only skill with no executable code. It reads TASKS.md through Claude Code's standard file access.

faq.question: Why was my edit blocked?
faq.answer: Your TASKS.md has an implementation task marked complete while the corresponding test task is still incomplete. Mark the test first, then the implementation.

faq.question: How is this different from test coverage tools?
faq.answer: Test coverage tools measure code percentage. This skill enforces task order in planning documents. Use both together for complete quality assurance.
